search_terms_used: pre-race taper training reduction endurance pubmed
tapering endurance athletes systematic review pubmed
taper duration optimal running performance pubmed
coverage_notes: Strong coverage: 2 meta-analyses (Bosquet 2007, Wang 2023), 3 RCTs (Houmard 1994, Skovgaard 2018, Mujika 2002), 1 controlled trial (Houmard 1990), 2 observational studies (Smyth 2021 158K runners, Spilsbury 2015 elite British), 2 comprehensive reviews (Mujika & Padilla 2003 landmark, Mujika 2004 physiological mechanisms). Running-specific in 8/10 papers. Covers optimal duration, volume reduction, intensity maintenance, frequency, taper shape, physiological mechanisms, and recreational vs elite differences.

papers.title: Effects of tapering on performance: a meta-analysis
papers.journal: Medicine and Science in Sports and Exercise
papers.notes: Meta-analysis of 27 studies finding a 2-week exponential taper with 41-60% volume reduction maximizes performance gains (~3% improvement).

papers.title: Effects of tapering on performance in endurance athletes: A systematic review and meta-analysis
papers.notes: Updated meta-analysis of 14 studies showing significant performance improvements from tapering; optimal taper <=21 days with 41-60% volume reduction and pre-taper overload enhancing effects.

papers.title: Longer Disciplined Tapers Improve Marathon Performance for Recreational Runners
papers.journal: Frontiers in Sports and Active Living
papers.notes: Analyzed 158,000+ recreational marathoners finding strict 3-week tapers associated with 5 min 32 s (2.6%) faster finish times vs minimal taper, with females benefiting more than males.

papers.title: The effects of taper on performance in distance runners
papers.journal: Medicine and Science in Sports and Exercise
papers.notes: RCT showing 7-day run taper with 85% volume reduction and maintained high-intensity intervals improved 5 km time by 3% and running economy.

papers.title: Effect of tapering after a period of high-volume sprint interval training on running performance and muscular adaptations in moderately trained runners
papers.notes: Examined 18-day taper following 40 days of high-volume sprint interval training in moderately trained runners, finding improved short-duration running capacity and increased muscle Na+/K+ ATPase expression.

papers.title: Reduced training maintains performance in distance runners
papers.notes: Showed 70% reduction in training volume over 3 weeks maintained 5 km race performance, VO2max, and muscular power, with time to exhaustion increasing 9.5% by week 3.

papers.title: Physiological and performance responses to a 6-day taper in middle-distance runners: influence of training frequency
papers.notes: Compared daily training vs resting every 3rd day during a 6-day taper in middle-distance runners, finding that maintaining daily frequency produced significant 800m performance gains while reduced frequency did not.

papers.title: Tapering strategies in elite British endurance runners
papers.notes: Documented pre-competition tapering practices of elite British endurance runners across middle-distance, long-distance, and marathon events, characterizing taper duration, volume reduction, and intensity patterns by event distance.

papers.title: Scientific bases for precompetition tapering strategies
papers.journal: Medicine and Science in Sports and Exercise
papers.notes: Landmark review defining taper as progressive nonlinear training load reduction, recommending 60-90% volume reduction, no more than 20% frequency reduction, maintained intensity, 4-28 day duration, yielding ~3% performance improvement.

papers.title: Physiological changes associated with the pre-event taper in athletes
papers.notes: Comprehensive review cataloguing physiological changes during taper including increased muscle glycogen, hormonal shifts, improved muscle contractile properties, enhanced oxidative enzyme activity, and reduced mood disturbance.
